title: Who needs urgent referral
html: Arrange same-day secondary care assessment if there is jaundice with synthetic dysfunction, decompensation features (ascites, encephalopathy, bleeding), suspected malignancy pattern (e.g. weight loss with cholestatic jaundice), or marked liver test derangement.

title: Initial primary care assessment
html:
• History: alcohol, medicines/supplements, metabolic risk, viral risk factors, symptoms.
  • Examination: chronic liver disease stigmata, jaundice, hepatomegaly, fluid overload, encephalopathy signs.
  • Repeat and extend bloods where indicated, including synthetic markers and platelets.

title: Threshold-based follow-up
html:
• <2× ULN and stable: repeat and review reversible causes.
  • >2–3× ULN: investigate promptly and consider specialist input.
  • Persistently abnormal without explanation: refer.
